channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Tesla Settles Some Worker Racism Claims as Bigger Trial Looms
item.description: reached an agreement to settle several workers’ claims alleging pervasive anti-Black racism at one of its California plants, while the company still faces a jury trial in a broader case brought by the state’s civil rights enforcement agency. Related: Tesla’s Musk Expects Widespread US Use of Cars Without Human Monitors This Year Tesla has lost arbitration proceedings in some cases and privately settled others while publicly denying wrongdoing. Five years ago, a similar case generated one of the largest-ever verdicts in a discrimination case involving a single worker—$137 million—but Tesla got the award reduced 98% before reaching a confidential settlement.

item.title: Deadly Screwworm Parasite Found in US Threatens Cattle Herd
item.description: A deadly cattle parasite was confirmed in the U.S. The detection of the parasite, which is deadly to cattle, comes at a dire time for the U.S. beef and cattle industry, with a smaller herd having already sent consumer prices to record highs.

item.title: Bending the Casualty Curve: Why Casualty Analytics Is Approaching Its Inflection Point
item.description: D’Arcy “Casualty Catastrophe Analytics: Where We Are Now and Where We Should Be on This Critical Risk,” Variance, Vol. “The immense diversity of the causes of casualty catastrophes as well as a lack of historical antecedents makes modeling casualty cats more difficult than modeling property cats, but not impossible.” — Stephen P.

item.title: Lockton hires Marsh veteran AnnMarie Rodgers as national corporate head in Australia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Exclusive: Scotch Raises $20M Series A To Disrupt Legacy Liquor Retail Tech With AI
item.description: Scotch , an AI-native operating system designed specifically for liquor store owners, has secured $20 million in a Series A funding round, the company tells Crunchbase News exclusively. Operating as an “all-in-one” software ecosystem, Scotch provides liquor retailers with point-of-sale hardware, custom software, payment processing and a back-office suite to manage state-by-state regulatory complexities. VMG Partners led Scotch’s Series A raise, which included participation from First Round Capital , Lerer Hippeau and Toba Capital .

item.title: Active Startup Investors Didn’t Hold Back In May
item.description: startup investors are putting more money to work than ever, but they’re concentrating capital into a smaller number of deals. That trend is reflected in the May tallies for most active investors. For a more granular view, we broke out ranks below by category, including active lead backers, highest spending investors, and most prolific venture dealmakers.

item.title: Arthur Re platform streamlined index-trigger cat bond issuance for Oak Global: Gallagher Re
item.description: Gallagher Re explained that the cat bond enhances Oak’s “underwriting portfolio with diversified capital markets capacity.” Jason Bolding, Global CEO, Gallagher Securities, commented on the deal and its use of the new platform, “Quercian Re 2026-1 demonstrates the strength of Arthur Re as a platform to deliver faster, more efficient index catastrophe bond issuance, enhancing how we connect clients with capital markets.” Paddy Ellis, Global Head of Retrocession, Gallagher Re, added, “This transaction highlights how Gallagher Re is product agnostic, integrating innovative retrocession solutions such as Arthur Re, and accessing the most suitable and cost-efficient capital for our clients.” platform for index-trigger catastrophe bond transactions demonstrated how it can deliver on efficiency and speed to market with the recent $150 million Arthur Re Ltd. Gallagher Securities acted as the sole structuring agent and sole bookrunner for the debut Oak Global cat bond, Quercian Re 2026-1 which is the first transaction executed using and issued by Arthur Re Ltd.

item.title: Earthquake models fail to factor in events driving two-thirds of recent industry losses: MS Amlin
item.description: Catastrophe models have been called out as failing to account for so-called “supershear” earthquake events, with specialist Lloyd’s re/insurer MS Amlin stating that models “are failing to factor in a little-known class of earthquake despite it being linked to two thirds of industry losses from seismic events in the last decade.” For those underwriting insurance and reinsurance, or investing in catastrophe bonds and insurance-linked securities (ILS) with exposure to earthquake risk, this means there is a “blind spot” on potential major earthquake loss events, MS Amlin’s research suggests. Yet these types of earthquake events are “absent from seismic hazard models, building design codes and insurance catastrophe models,” MS Amlin says. MS Amlin’s researchers warn of a narrow window to resolve this issue before the next major California earthquake occurs and the new generation of quake models emerge.

item.title: Cedar Trace hires Chris Wilson from Artex, strengthening insurance-linked securities team
item.description: Cedar Trace, the Bermuda-based insurance, reinsurance, and asset management group, has announced the hiring of well-known industry executive Chris Wilson to its insurance-linked securities leadership, further strengthening its team and bringing meaningful operation experience in ILS. Chris Wilson joins as a Managing Director of Cedar Trace Capital Management and will be based in Bermuda for the company. In that position he played a key role in Artex Capital Solutions management of insurance-linked securities and reinsurance structures, as well as in facilitating both cedent and investor access to ILS capital and opportunities.

item.title: Legacy and retrospective reinsurance become a strategic capital tool for cedants: Howden Re
item.description: The use of legacy and retrospective reinsurance has evolved from operational relief to capital and financial target optimisation tools for cedants, Alex Roth, Head of Capital & Operational Solutions, International at Howden Re, highlighted in a recent commentary. Over the past decades, the legacy and retrospective reinsurance market operated under a relatively narrow set of use cases. Cedants viewed it primarily as a tactical tool for operational relief, a way to offload claims handling for closed portfolios, streamline legal entities, or exit non-core business lines.

item.title: Service-level agreement exposure among most significant risks facing data centres: Parametrix
item.description: Lloyd’s of London coverholder Parametrix has highlighted in a new white paper that service-level agreement (SLA) exposure is among the most significant operational risks facing data centres, noting that uptime, availability, latency, power, cooling and network performance commitments can translate into material financial exposure. “This shift makes SLAs a central component of the asset’s risk profile, with even brief disruptions able to trigger service credits, termination rights, and cash flow volatility under SLAs,” Parametrix explained The new white paper suggested that a 45-minute outage of a 100 MW data centre with $144 million in annual rent could result in a service credit loss of $24 million, reducing annual cash flow by approximately 42%. Parametrix continued, “That has made SLA exposure one of the largest concerns for data centre operators, brokers, insurers, investors, and lenders.

item.title: The Fidelis Partnership launches Guy Carpenter-placed PVT Consortium
item.description: The Fidelis Partnership (TFP) has launched a PVT Consortium, placed by Guy Carpenter, bringing together leading Lloyd’s syndicates to deploy new capacity into the war, terror and political violence (WTPV) market. Billy Ayres, Head of Underwriting – Crisis Management, The Fidelis Partnership, commented, “The launch of the TFP PVT Consortium is a direct expression of our high-conviction approach and our belief that the right response to uncertainty is to lean in, not step back. According to TFP, the Consortium will bring together capacity from leading Lloyd’s Syndicates, including Argenta, alongside TFP’s own Syndicates, 3123 and 2126.

item.title: Tesla Settles Some Worker Racism Claims From California Plant
item.description: reached an agreement to settle several workers’ claims alleging pervasive anti-Black racism at one of its California plants, while the company still faces a jury trial in a broader case brought by the state’s civil rights enforcement agency. For almost a decade, the company headed by Elon Musk has been trying to fend off allegations that the assembly line in Tesla’s Fremont plant was a hostile work environment filled with racist slurs, graffiti and drawings of nooses and swastikas. Related: Tesla’s Musk Expects Widespread US Use of Cars Without Human Monitors This Year Tesla has lost arbitration proceedings in some cases and privately settled others while publicly denying wrongdoing.

item.title: Zurich Insurance Expands Data-Center Offering Beyond the US
item.description: Zurich Insurance Group Plc is widening its offering for data-center projects to Europe and South America as it seeks to profit from the global buildout of infrastructure linked to artificial intelligence. Insurers like Zurich have been quick to offer risk coverage around data centers as they seek new streams of revenue at a time when their traditional businesses in property and casualty insurance face a slowdown in premium growth. Zurich says it underwrote more than 245 data-center construction projects in the US in 2025 and the business bumped its first-quarter earnings.

item.title: Beware Business IT Consultant Who Secretly Installs Remote Access to Network
item.description: Billie Conley, Jr., formerly of Shelton, pleaded guilty in federal court in New Haven to secretly giving himself remote access to the network of a business that hired him as a consultant. A Connecticut information technology consultant has pleaded guilty to fraud stemming from a computer intrusion scheme that cost a small business, according to federal prosecutors. He used this access to cause network problems and steal from the firm’s bank account, according to prosecutors.

item.title: TrueCar to build new insurance vertical
item.description: Car buying platform TrueCar, which was taken private earlier this year, announced that it will capitalize on “untapped opportunities” by expanding its OEM relationships and insurance offerings. As part of the effort, the company hired former Tesla and Marsh executive Nik Huyeng to lead the insurance and ancillary business, developing and bringing to market digitally integrated insurance solutions to help customers secure the right protection “while saving time and money.” Huyeng has more than 13 years of experience at the intersection of automotive, insurance, and financial services, and has spent four years at Tesla. At Tesla, he built it’s European insurance company from the ground up, securing regulatory licensing across the EU and UK, “and spearheaded the 2019 launch of Tesla Insurance in California.” Earlier in his career, Huyeng held roles at Marsh across Hong Kong, Beijing, and Asia Pacific, where he developed Tesla’s first dedicated auto insurance program globally and supported Marsh’s appointment as Tesla’s global insurance broker.

item.title: The Plaintiff Bar Brought AI to the Claim File. Carriers Should Arm the Adjuster.
item.description: The post The Plaintiff Bar Brought AI to the Claim File. A plaintiff-side model can ingest a claim file and flag the missing certified-mail trigger, the boilerplate reservation of rights , or the undocumented partial denial in seconds, then build the bad-faith narrative around it. What the industry should do next The plaintiff bar has already made its move, and it made it on the correspondence layer, because that is where carriers were slowest to modernize and most exposed when they got a word or a date wrong.

item.title: PayPay to enter life insurance
item.description: Japanese mobile payment service PayPay is buying a 70.2% stake in the life insurance unit of T&D Holdings for ¥134.3 billion ($840 million). The SoftBank-backed company, which has 70 million users, will begin joint initiatives toward making Taiyo Life insurance products available within the PayPay app. T&D Holdings owns several life insurance companies including Taiyo Life and Daido Life Insurance.

item.title: «No podemos perder el 50% del talento en un momento en el que el liderazgo es más importante que nunca»
item.description: La transformación tecnológica, la inteligencia artificial, los nuevos modelos de negocio y la creciente exigencia de los clientes están redefiniendo el futuro del sector asegurador. Sin embargo, para Juan Estallo, CEO de Helvetia Caser, el verdadero desafío no está en la tecnología, sino en las personas.

item.title: Markel España conmemora el 15º aniversario de su oficina de Barcelona
item.description: Markel España ha reunido a más de 160 profesionales del sector asegurador, principalmente corredores, en un evento para celebrar el 15º aniversario de su oficina de Barcelona. El director general de la entidad, Esteban Manzano, y el director de la oficina, Carlos de Puig, repasaron la trayectoria de la delegación desde su apertura en 2011.

item.title: Names Europe nombra a Jordi Pozo como nuevo director de Suscripción
item.description: Names Europe ha anunciado la incorporación de Jordi Pozo como CUO. Tiene una destacada trayectoria en diversas compañías, como Generali y RSA. Además, cuenta con un perfil orientado a resultados, desarrollo de negocio y trabajo en equipo.

item.title: Travelers: Vendor Issues Over Half of Wedding Insurance Claims in 2025
item.description: Vendor-related issues continued to be the top cause of claims paid on wedding insurance policies, according to data out this week from Travelers. For the fifth straight year, vendor-related issues were the leading cause of paid claims in 2025, coming in at 55% of total wedding claims. released its wedding insurance claims data for 2025.

item.title: OpenAI CEO to Share Oversight Ideas in Wake of Trump AI Order
item.description: While OpenAI praised Trump’s executive order for laying out a closer relationship between AI labs and government agencies, company staff including Chief Global Affairs Officer Chris Lehane cautioned Wednesday that many details on the directive remain unclear. officials, just a day after President Donald Trump called for AI companies to voluntarily share cutting-edge models for government review before their release. During a briefing with reporters in Washington, where he unveiled a new policy agenda, Lehane stressed that Altman would be discussing OpenAI’s ideas for how AI companies should work with the government during those White House meetings.

item.title: British Lawmaker Sues Musk’s xAI Over Sexualized Grok Images
item.description: British lawmaker Jess Asato is suing Elon Musk’s xAI, saying in a statement on Wednesday the Grok AI platform had been used to create fake sexualized images of her. Grok, distributed through Musk’s social media platform X, is currently subject to regulatory probes in several countries after an outcry earlier this year over its use to create non-consensual sexualized images. The statement from Asato’s office said that after she condemned Grok in January, users created and shared fake images depicting her in a bikini and a video showing her “being chloroformed and prepared for a sexual assault.” In March, the City of Baltimore sued xAI, claiming the Grok’s ability to create fake sexualized images violated the city’s consumer protection law.

item.title: Mistuio Sumitomo, Aioi Nissay target US$1.75bn profit by FY30 after merger
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allianz Trade appoints Ricky Hung to lead Hong Kong distribution
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Munich Re strengthens cyber team with dual leadership appointments for APAC, Africa
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA launches investigation into second motor finance claims management company
item.description: The FCA has opened an enforcement investigation into Consultation Claims Limited (CCL) following concerns about its conduct in the period April 2025 to December 2025 in relation to motor finance claims. If those customers are not happy with the firm’s response, they should complain to the Claims Management Ombudsman. Announcing the investigation allows consumers who may have unknowingly been signed up or who may have been presented with documents purporting to be signed by them when they have not, to complain to CCL.

item.title: Gothaer seeks €100m German flood reinsurance with debut Yardstick Re catastrophe bond
item.description: Gothaer Allgemeine Versicherungs AG, a German insurance company, has entered the catastrophe bond market for its debut sponsorship, targeting €100 million or more in reinsurance limit to cover flood events in Germany with a Yardstick Re DAC (Series 2026-1) cat bond issuance, Artemis can report. This is the first catastrophe bond that is solely focused on flood risks in Germany and sees Gothaer Allgemeine Versicherungs AG (Gothaer), a non-life property and casualty focused insurance company focused on private client and business coverage and part of the BarmeniaGothaer Group, targeting cat bond coverage for the first time as well. The currently €100 million tranche of Series 2026-1 notes that Yardstick Re DAC is offering are designed to provide insurer Gothaer with four years of reinsurance protection to cover certain losses from major flood events in Germany.

item.title: CEA’s risk transfer grew to $8.2bn at Apr 30th. New cat bond maintains ILS market share
item.description: As of April 30th 2026, the catastrophe bond market was providing 35% of the CEA’s risk transfer limit, a slight reduction in share since the end of 2025 but still at the highest amount of cat bond limit the CEA has had in-force. When we last reported on this significant buyer of risk transfer, the California Earthquake Authority (CEA) had grown the tower slightly to $7.912 billion as of the end of 2025, with the catastrophe bond market providing its largest share of the risk transfer limit ever, at 36% of the total. This exposure growth has driven a need for more financing and at April 30th 2026 the reinsurance and cat bond risk transfer tower of over $8.2 billion has grown by more than 5% year-on-year.

item.title: Pinnacle announces recent acquisitions including Bermuda-based Ordinance
item.description: has announced the acquisition of Bermuda-based Ordinance Holdings Limited and the renewal rights for clients of Canadian firm Grape Bay Actuarial Consulting Inc. According to the announcement, Julie Perron, the president of Ordinance and Grape Bay, will remain based in Canada and work with Pinnacle as an independent contractor to serve former clients. “This acquisition of Ordinance and clients of Grape Bay will increase Pinnacle’s international presence and enhance the services we provide our clients in Bermuda and Canada.

item.title: GIFT IFSC continues rapid growth as registrations and re/insurance activity rise
item.description: IFSCA said these trends reflect sustained traction across insurance and reinsurance activity within GIFT IFSC, supported by continued onboarding of participants and rising business volumes, pointing to deepening market depth. The latest quarterly bulletin from the International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) has highlighted the continued expansion of India’s Gujarat International Finance Tec-City International Financial Services Centre (GIFT IFSC), describing it as a transformative element in the country’s financial sector strategy and its integration with global markets. Positioned as a hub for global financial and technology services, GIFT IFSC provides a specialised ecosystem for conducting international financial transactions from within India.

item.title: Resolution Life strengthens Asian presence with three new reinsurance agreements
item.description: Resolution Life, an international life insurance group focused on reinsurance and the management and acquisition of life insurance portfolios, has completed three new transactions across Asia. The agreements, which involve life insurance business in Hong Kong and Japan, reflect Resolution Life’s ongoing involvement in supporting insurers across the region and its continued expansion within key Asian markets. Through these agreements, Resolution Life assumes market, mortality and policyholder behaviour risks, enabling its insurer partners to support the long-term sustainability of policyholder dividends.

item.title: SpaghettiOs Infested With Worms or Parasites, Lawsuit Claims
item.description: A Florida mother and her daughter sued Campbell’s after eating SpaghettiOs that they claim were contaminated by worms or parasites, and therefore unfit to eat. There “appeared to be worms or parasites actively moving within the food,” and Hubbard shot videos that “clearly depict worm-like organisms moving within the food product,” the complaint said. In a complaint filed on Tuesday, Mary Hubbard said she discovered the alleged contamination around June 6, 2024, after she and her daughter, known as P.L., began eating SpaghettiOs at their home in Okeechobee County.

item.title: Automakers, Retailers Warn Memory-Chip Shortage Is Impacting Prices
item.description: Reuters reported in December that the acute global shortage of memory chips is forcing AI and consumer electronics companies to fight for dwindling supplies, as prices soar for the unglamorous but essential components that allow devices to store data. consumer goods and disrupt supply chains, groups representing automakers, retailers, electronic firms and others said on Wednesday. government has dedicated billions in subsidies to boost memory-chip production, including at Micron.

item.title: Trump Scraps Ocean Sensors Providing Crucial Data on Climate, Flooding
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to dismantle most of a $386 million federal ocean-observing network will leave scientists without an irreplaceable source of data used to understand how climate change is affecting crucial currents and marine ecosystems and increasing coastal flooding. The network of sensors, moorings and autonomous instruments known as the Ocean Observatories Initiative will be stripped of almost all in-water infrastructure, according to plans announced in late May by the National Science Foundation. The move follows a multi-pronged attack by the Trump administration on climate-related research and programs, as well as previous efforts to reduce funding for the initiative.

item.title: Insurers Must Plan for Tech’s Unintended Consequences
item.description: Many insurers moved workloads to cloud providers because the cloud was perceived to be cheaper. Every decision has consequences, which is clearly the point. A problem or opportunity is recognized, a plan is developed, resources are deployed and action is taken.

item.title: AIG’s Turnaround Under Zaffino Sets Stage for New Leadership
item.description: Thanks to a dramatic turnaround under his predecessor, Peter Zaffino, American International Group Inc. Nine of the 14 people who made up Zaffino’s bench of top deputies just over two years ago are no longer on the executive leadership team. Lauded in the industry for a hard-working ethos and attention to detail, Zaffino, 59, has also perturbed senior executives during his five-year tenure.

item.title: Marsh hires Christopher Cheung in senior financial lines role
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘There is no stopping it’: game changing AI set to outgrow cyber, presents opportunity for insurers
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Markel appoints Matthew Stock to lead new Australian fine art and specie offering
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Los defraudadores también se preparan para el Hot Sale y Black Friday
item.description: Campañas especiales, como el Hot Sale o Black Friday , elevan las compras digitales y, con ellas, los intentos de fraude. Para los defraudadores, la combinación de promociones, urgencia y un mayor volumen de transacciones es un gran atractivo para poner en marcha campañas de phishing , una situación que obliga a los bancos y fintechs a reforzar sus controles de prevención. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: ACORD Offers Free Access to Transcriber
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) ACORD has introduced complimentary access to ACORD Transcriber for members of eligible ACORD programs. “This new member benefit will help them to maximize the value of their ACORD Forms data, liberating it from the page and feeding it directly into digital systems.” ACORD Transcriber is an intelligent document processing tool offered by ACORD Solutions Group. Eligible ACORD members can use ACORD Transcriber to extract data from up to 50,000 pages a year from ACORD Forms.

item.title: AXIS Names Christinna Geller Financial Lines Head
item.description: (Christina Geller, Head of Wholesale Financial Lines, North America, AXIS Capital Holdings.) AXIS Capital Holdings Limited (Pembroke, Bermuda) has promoted Christina Geller to the role of Head of Wholesale Financial Lines in North America, effective June 16. Geller will report to John Van Decker, Head of Global Financial Lines, AXIS. John Van Decker, Head of Global Financial Lines, AXIS.

item.title: AI-Native Insurance Demands Stronger Digital Foundations
item.description: AI-native insurance is not just about deploying new technologies. (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) As insurers accelerate toward AI-native operating models, the conversation is often framed around algorithms, models and use cases. To deliver on the promise of agentic AI, insurers must first redefine the digital core that supports it.

item.title: Car Owners Shocked by $200 Gas Bills Finally Embrace Used EVs
item.description: Flickinger is one of the growing number of Americans finding refuge from soaring fuel prices in a used-car market that’s awash in affordable EVs that automakers struggled to sell at higher prices when they were new. Close to 40% of used battery-powered cars currently on dealer lots are priced below $25,000, according to researcher Cox Automotive, roughly half what the average new car sells for in the US. And more than a million are set to hit the used car lot by late next year, often with years of coverage remaining on their original factory warranty, assuaging concerns about the longevity of EV batteries that have held back some first-time EV shoppers.

item.title: Natural-Disaster Insurance Gap Now Tops $420B Globally, Swiss Re Says
item.description: The value of uninsured natural-catastrophe losses rose more than 7% globally last year to $424 billion, with North America the most exposed region, according to a fresh analysis. While insurance coverage has broadly kept pace with rising catastrophe exposure, the protection gap—in absolute terms—has gone up as the value of exposed assets has grown, the Swiss Re Institute said on Wednesday. In emerging markets, resilience to natural catastrophes has declined over the decade, which means these regions are currently accumulating risk as insurance coverage declines, according to the report.

item.title: Multiple Factors Affect Auto, Workers Comp Claim Complexity: Report
item.description: Though workers comp and auto remain profitable lines within the insurance industry, there a number of challenges to monitor, according to Enlyte’s 2026 Envision Trends Report, which explores the forces driving claim complexity. The report highlights how utilization, treatment timing, and pricing dynamics are converging to increase severity across workers’ comp and auto claims. Factors including litigation, workforce transformation, and evolving medical and vehicle challenges are reshaping claims management while insurers and employers face mounting pressure from rising severity, longer claim duration, and expanding operational demands, the annual report outlined.

item.title: People Moves: Arch Capital Expands Rajeh’s Role as President as Gansberg Steps Down
item.description: David Gansberg is stepping down from his role as a president of Arch and departing the company following a distinguished tenure, having played an important role in building the organization to its current position. Rajeh, who most recently oversaw Arch’s Reinsurance and Mortgage segments, will also take on responsibility for Arch’s Insurance segment as the company moves forward under a single president model. , the Bermuda-based re/insurer, announced the expansion of Maamoun Rajeh’s role as president.

item.title: Convex Launches Tech-Enabled MGA Called Kinetic Insurance Services
item.description: Convex Group Ltd., the international specialty insurer and reinsurer, announced the launch of Kinetic Insurance Services , a wholly owned, technology-enabled managing general agency, which will start trading in Q4 2026. The MGA has received UK regulatory approval and will be led by Theo Butt, former CEO of Convex Insurance UK Ltd. UK-based Kinetic will target specialty and niche lines of business complementary to Convex’s existing portfolio.
